signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Cooling US Labor Market
signals.text: GBP/USD trades near 1.3365 as the G10 currency strength scorecard flags a critical bilateral divergence. GBP is the strongest G10 currency this week with a 1W average of +0.801% across 4 pairs, while the USD is the weakest at -0.778% across 7 pairs, generating a 1.579% leader-to-laggard spread. This divergence is driven by cooling US labor market data following weak NFP, which offsets the neutral carry profile of BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% (0bp SONIA-SOFR spread). Speculative positioning remains supportive with GBP COT net long +631 (50.3% long) vs USD net short -5,369. The options market, however, maintains a moderate put skew with 25d RR at -0.80, signaling hedging against GBP upside exhaustion. Continued US data misses will solidify the breakout toward 1.3500.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Geopolitical Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 161.11, holding above the critical 160.00 threshold where verbal and physical BoJ intervention risks escalate. The pair is structurally supported by a +268bp carry advantage (SOFR 3.66% vs TONA 0.98%), which dominates the normal volatility regime (VIX at 16.1). However, geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and a crowded speculative short position (JPY COT net short -115,033, 28.4% long) elevate unwind risks. The options market is actively pricing downside protection, with the 25d RR showing a moderate put skew of -1.09 and a notable vol risk premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.6%, +3.6% VRP). A break below 160.00 targets 158.50 as intervention fears or geopolitical escalation trigger JPY short covering.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.6936, supported by a -69bp carry advantage over the USD (SOFR 3.66% vs AONIA 4.35%). Speculative positioning is heavily long with AUD COT net long +29,035 (64.9% long), but the options market is pricing in extreme risk. The AUD/USD vol risk premium has surged to +17.6% (IV 24.7% vs HV30 7.1%), indicating massive demand for downside protection despite the spot recovery. This is confirmed by a moderate put skew of -0.89 in the 25d RR. The divergence between bullish speculative positioning and defensive options pricing suggests a fragile trend. Watch for a break of 0.6900 to trigger a rapid long-unwind toward 0.6820.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Sentiment Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4172, supported by (SOFR 3.66% vs CORRA 2.34%) and a heavily short speculative profile (CAD COT net short -87,096, 20.6% long). Retail sentiment has reached an extreme 92% short bias, setting up a powerful contrarian long signal as retail traders attempt to fade the institutional trend. Additionally, the historical relationship with oil has broken down, with the USD/CAD vs WTI correlation collapsing to -0.866 vs a 252d norm of +0.013 (z = -2.07σ). This decoupling means falling oil prices are no longer the primary driver, leaving the pair to be guided by the wide carry spread and retail capitulation risk. A sustained hold above 1.4200 targets 1.4350.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CESI Weakness + Lagarde Defense
signals.text: EUR/USD trades at 1.1447, softening below 1.1450 as softer Eurozone inflation data trims ECB hike expectations. This is reinforced by a strong negative Economic Surprise Index (CESI net -16 misses), indicating persistent data disappointment. While Lagarde continues to defend the June rate hike due to core inflation and supply shocks, the ECB's real policy rate of +0.03% (2.25% rate - 2.22% inflation expectations) remains highly accommodative compared to the Fed's restrictive +1.49% real rate. This real rate deficit, combined with a +148bp carry disadvantage (SOFR 3.66% vs €STR 2.18%), keeps EUR/USD upside structurally capped. Watch the next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul for any formal shift in policy guidance.
